pattern: Remapping every blacklisted value below a shrunken white-list boundary onto an unused value above it turns a sparse, unbounded exclusion set into a dense range a single bounded random draw can sample from.
hints: Rejection sampling (redraw until you miss the blacklist) is correct but can spin forever when the blacklist is dense — you need a scheme with a bounded number of random calls.
Shrink the sampling range to whitelist size m = n - blacklist.length; every value below m that IS blacklisted needs somewhere else to point.
Collect the unblacklisted values living at or above m (the 'tail') and map each low blacklisted value to one of them; then a single draw in [0, m) is always legal.

edge_inputs.description: blacklist covers the tail exactly, no remap needed
blacklist covers all but one value, output fully determined
blacklist scattered through both the white region and the tail
blacklist given out of order and with values clustered near n-1
large n with a small blacklist near the low end

stress.description: random n with a random-size blacklist and a burst of picks
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 500)
    k = rng.randint(0, max(0, n - 1))
    blacklist = rng.sample(range(n), k) if k > 0 else []
    ops = ['Solution']
    argv = [[n, blacklist]]
    calls = max(1, min(size, 200))
    for _ in range(calls):
        ops.append('pick')
        argv.append([])
    return [ops, argv]

complexity.time: O(b) to build, O(1) per pick
